title: Frequency of the Most Frequent Element

testcases.input: nums = [1,2,4], k = 5 Output: 3 Explanation: Increment the first element three times and the second element two times to make nums = [4,4,4]. 4 has a frequency of 3.
testcases.expectedOutput: 3 Explanation: Increment the first element three times and the second element two times to make nums = [4,4,4]. 4 has a frequency of 3.

testcases.input: nums = [1,4,8,13], k = 5 Output: 2 Explanation: There are multiple optimal solutions: - Increment the first element three times to make nums = [4,4,8,13]. 4 has a frequency of 2. - Increment the second element four times to make nums = [1,8,8,13]. 8 has a frequency of 2. - Increment the third element five times to make nums = [1,4,13,13]. 13 has a frequency of 2.
testcases.expectedOutput: 2 Explanation: There are multiple optimal solutions: - Increment the first element three times to make nums = [4,4,8,13]. 4 has a frequency of 2. - Increment the second element four times to make nums = [1,8,8,13]. 8 has a frequency of 2. - Increment the third element five times to make nums = [1,4,13,13]. 13 has a frequency of 2.
